_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to solve equations graphically or by systematic guess-check-and-refine, using digital tools.

_source.w: We are learning to solve equations graphically or by systematic guess-check-and-refine, using digital tools.
y: Not all equations can be solved algebraically — graphical and numerical methods provide powerful alternatives for finding approximate solutions.
Using digital tools for equation-solving reflects professional practice in science, engineering and data analysis.
The guess-check-refine strategy develops systematic thinking and an understanding of convergence.
eg: Using a graphing tool to find the intersection of y = x³ and y = 3x + 2.
Applying systematic guess-check-refine to solve x³ – x = 5 to 2 decimal places.
Finding the approximate solution to sin(x) = x/2 using a graphical approach.
Using technology to solve an equation arising from a real-world model.
Comparing graphical solutions with algebraic solutions to verify accuracy.
Discussing the limitations of numerical methods — approximate versus exact solutions.
